note: Complete timeline with all events from 1880-1950, displayed proportionally

extracted_data.description: Rabbi Yosef Yitzchak Schneersohn (the Rayatz) is born in Lubavitch. At his circumcision, his grandfather predicts the crying baby will grow up to be a Rebbe.

extractions.passage: June 30, 1880: Rabbi Yosef Yitzchak Schneersborn is born

extracted_data.description: Czar Alexander II is assassinated. His son Alexander III rolls back reforms and increases oppression of Russian Jews.

extractions.passage: 1881: Czar Alexander II of Russia is assassinated

extracted_data.description: Nechama Dina Schneersohn, future wife of the Rayatz, is born in Kishinev.

extractions.passage: October 24, 1881: Nechama Dina Schneersohn is born

extracted_data.description: Czar Alexander III institutes the May Laws, heavily restricting Jewish residency, economic growth, and education.

extractions.passage: 1882: Czar Alexander III institutes the May Laws

extracted_data.description: The early Russian Zionist group BILU sends first settlers to Eretz Yisrael.
Rabbi Shmuel implies his passing is imminent, telling his wife he will 'rise up thirty-two steps in a spiritual way'.

extractions.passage: Erev Rosh HaShanah 1882: Rabbi Shmuel predicts his passing

extracted_data.description: Three days after Yom Kippur, Rabbi Shmuel passes away in Lubavitch at age forty-eight.
Chabad elders convene and implore the Rebbe's sons to assume leadership. They initially refuse but consent to recite maamarim privately.
extracted_data.title_en: Leadership Conclave after Rabbi Shmuel's Passing

extracted_data.description: Two-year-old Yosef Yitzchak watches his uncle Mendel's wedding. During mourning, his mother brings him to his father's study where he is moved by intense praying.
The Rashab returns to Lubavitch after medical treatment, so changed that young Yosef Yitzchak doesn't recognize him.
At age four, Yosef Yitzchak begins formal education under Rabbi Yekusiel Aizik.
Rabbi Shmuel Betzalel Sheftel (Rashbatz), future teacher of the Rayatz, relocates to Bulharkov.
Five-year-old Yosef Yitzchak travels with parents to Yalta for six months, taught by Rabbi Schneur Zalman Slonim.
extracted_data.title_en: Move to Yalta for Medical Treatment

extracted_data.description: During Lag BaOmer gathering in Kharkiv, Yosef Yitzchak notices his mother crying and climbs through crowd to inform his father.
At age seven, Yosef Yitzchak begins studying with Rabbi Shimshon, memorizing two orders of Mishnah.
The Rashab and wife frequently seek medical treatment, leaving Yosef Yitzchak lonely. He finds solace with elderly Chasidim and begins recording chasidic lore.
extracted_data.title_en: Period of Loneliness and Diary Writing

extracted_data.description: The Rashab, concerned about his health, writes an ethical will outlining how his wife should educate their son.
Samuel Polyakov, the 'rail king of Russia,' passes away.
The Rashab and wife return to Lubavitch. Nine-year-old Yosef Yitzchak feels renewed maturity and begins facing his father during maamarim.
The Rashab officially begins receiving Chasidim for yechidut (private meetings).
Ten-year-old Yosef Yitzchak sneaks out in snowstorm to the Ohel to pray for his father's recovery. Fever miraculously breaks.
extracted_data.title_en: Yosef Yitzchak's Prayer at the Ohel

extracted_data.description: During yarmarka, Yosef Yitzchak operates microloan fund and is wrongfully arrested but released after defending a butcher.
The Rashab formally begins studying chasidic philosophy with Yosef Yitzchak.
extracted_data.title_en: Rashab Studies Chasidic Philosophy with Son

extracted_data.description: On June 26, Yosef Yitzchak celebrates his bar mitzvah, memorizing three maamarim and reciting the third by heart.
Rabbi Zalman Aharon relocates to Vitebsk, deferring leadership to his younger brother the Rashab.
On Rosh HaShanah, the Rashab fully accepts position of Rebbe, taking his father's seat after eleven years.
Czar Alexander III dies, succeeded by Nicholas II who continues anti-Jewish policies.
During summer in Bolivke, the Rashab dedicates time to his fifteen-year-old son's development. On his birthday, takes him to the Ohel for sacred covenant (akedah).
extracted_data.title_en: Summer Mentoring in Bolivke and Akedah

extracted_data.description: The Rayatz becomes engaged to Nechama Dina, celebration lasts a week.
extracted_data.title_en: Engagement of the Rayatz and Nechama Dina

extracted_data.description: On August 22 (13 Elul), the Rayatz marries Nechama Dina Schneersohn.
extracted_data.title_en: Wedding of the Rayatz and Nechama Dina

extracted_data.description: On September 12 (15 Elul), at wedding reception, the Rashab announces revolutionary plan to open yeshivah integrating Talmud and chasidic philosophy.
On September 15 (18 Elul), studies officially begin for first group of yeshivah students under Rabbi Shmuel Gronem.
Eighteen-year-old Yosef Yitzchak is appointed executive director of yeshivah, begins fundraising and institutes rigorous standards.
On Simchat Torah, the Rashab officially names the institution Yeshivas Tomchei Temimim and students are titled temimim.
On Simchat Torah, the Rashab lays out mission statement comparing students to King David's soldiers, tasking them with disseminating Chasidism.
The Rayatz opens textile mill in Dubrovno using diverted JCA funds, providing Shabbat-observant employment for 2000 Jews.
The Rayatz is arrested for second time due to accusations by secular school teachers.
War breaks out between Japan and Russia, thousands of Jewish soldiers enlist.
March 1906: Poalei Tzion members march into yeshivah. Days later, 200 return with weapons, shoot at yeshivah.
Summer 1906: Poalei Tzion raids Rashab's summer home in Bolivke with revolvers while children present.
December 1906: The Rayatz is investigated and arrested due to riots, though proves yeshivah wasn't involved.
The Rashab purchases Beit Romano in Hebron, laying foundation to establish Toras Emes yeshivah.
The Rashab sends students led by Rabbi Zalman Havlin to Hebron to establish Toras Emes.
World War I breaks out. Rebbetzin Rivkah passes away. JDC is established.
In late October, the Rashab and Rayatz depart Lubavitch due to advancing German army, ending 102-year history as Chabad capital.
The Bolshevik Revolution takes place. Czar Nicholas II abdicates, ending Pale of Settlement.
March 21 (2 Nisan): The Rashab passes away in Rostov. The Rayatz accepts position of Sixth Lubavitcher Rebbe.
January: Bolsheviks take Rostov, the Rashab curtails public activities. February: Predicts 'dark clouds' for 22 years.
July: Yevesektzia officers attempt to arrest Rebbe during prayers, interrogate but release him.
February: Yevesektzia stages show trial against yeshivah. Secret police raids Rebbe's home, imprisons future son-in-law.
February 15: Just after midnight, GPU officers arrest Rebbe, sentence him to death by firing squad.
July 12 (12 Tamuz): Rebbe informed of release from exile. Returns to Leningrad two days later.
October 21: Rebbe departs Russia by train, taking historic library, arrives to hero's welcome in Riga.
July: Rebbe publishes first maamar since leaving Russia, printed in Yiddish to broaden dissemination.
November 27 (14 Kislev): 'Royal Wedding' of Rebbe's daughter Chaya Mushka to Ramash takes place at yeshivah in Warsaw.
extracted_data.title_en: Wedding of Ramash and Chaya Mushka

extracted_data.description: September 17: Rebbe arrives in New York Harbor to crowd of thousands.
July 17: Rebbe departs US for Europe after meeting President Hoover.
March 19: Rebbe arrives in NY, declares 'America iz nisht aderesh' and announces opening of American Yeshivas Tomchei Temimim.
August 16 (12 Av): Agudas Chassidei Chabad purchases 770 Eastern Parkway as Rebbe's permanent residence.
September 13: Rebbe's youngest daughter Sheina is murdered in Treblinka death camp.
November 5: Rebbe's son-in-law Mendel Horenstein is murdered in Treblinka.
December 2: A dedicated building is purchased for growing Chabad yeshivah in Montreal.
December 2: Massive transport of 232 Chasidim crosses Russian border into Poland. Rebbe senses crossing spiritually.
Following State of Israel, Rebbe directs founding of Kfar Chabad near Tel Aviv for Russian refugees.
March 17: Rebbe and family granted US Citizenship at 770 Eastern Parkway.
January 28: At 7:45 a.m. on Shabbat, Rebbe suffers final heart attack and passes away at 7:50 a.m.
